title: Insecure Pseudo-Random Number Generator
description: Use of non-cryptographic PRNGs (Math.random, rand(), random.random) for security-sensitive operations (token generation, nonces, key material) violates SOC2 CC6.1. These generators are predictable.

finding.message: Insecure PRNG '{{token}}' used in security context in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Use a cryptographically secure RNG: os.urandom(), secrets module (Python), crypto.randomBytes() (Node.js), rand::thread_rng() with OsRng (Rust), java.security.SecureRandom (Java).
Never use Math.random(), rand(), or random.random() for tokens, nonces, salts, or session IDs.
NIST SP 800-90A defines approved DRBG constructs.
